title: network: add support for rootless network with slirp4netns
body:
slirp4netns is required to setup the network namespace:

https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body: /cc @AkihiroSuda

```console
$ bin/podman run --rm -ti alpine ifconfig
lo        Link encap:Local Loopback  
          inet addr:127.0.0.1  Mask:255.0.0.0
          inet6 addr: ::1/128 Scope:Host
          UP LOOPBACK RUNNING  MTU:65536  Metric:1
          RX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 frame:0
          TX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 carrier:0
          collisions:0 txqueuelen:1000 
          RX bytes:0 (0.0 B)  TX bytes:0 (0.0 B)

tap0      Link encap:Ethernet  HWaddr CA:8D:73:D0:A6:4A  
          inet addr:10.0.2.100  Bcast:10.0.2.255  Mask:255.255.255.0
          inet6 addr: fe80::c88d:73ff:fed0:a64a/64 Scope:Link
          UP BROADCAST RUNNING  MTU:1500  Metric:1
          RX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 frame:0
          TX packets:1 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 carrier:0
          collisions:0 txqueuelen:1000 
          RX bytes:0 (0.0 B)  TX bytes:90 (90.0 B)
```
@AkihiroSuda are you interested in getting this packaged for distributions?
it probably makes sense as part of containers-common, as we will also need to find a home for fuse-overlayfs

how about rootlesscontainers-common?

cc @cyphar

@AkihiroSuda @giuseppe Is there any use for this tool for an admin or is it more likely to be embeded in another tool like podman.  
I am wondering if it should be installed in /usr/bin and have man pages describing it, or installed in /usr/libexec/ like conmon is.
@rhatdan you could use it manually to setup a network namespace, I think it is similar to `newuidmap`/`newgidmap` that we use internally but still usable directly
Ok in that case we need at least a man page.  Putting it into containers-common is the easiest way forward.  @lsm5 WDYT?

containers-common seems including rhel-specific stuff? (/usr/share/rhel) how is it planned to be packaged for non-redhat distro?

https://fedora.pkgs.org/rawhide/fedora-x86_64/containers-common-0.1.31-9.gitae64ff7.fc29.x86_64.rpm.html

slirp4netns should be also useful for rootless moby, buildkit, img, and Kubernetes ("usernetes")

Maybe we can create a standalone rpm for slirp4netns, and then consider putting into other pkg?

@AkihiroSuda Well I am only talking about where we ship it.  Our version of containers-common has RHEL mounts but this does not need to be in SUSE or Ubuntu's version.
The other content is required like mounts.conf, registries.conf etc.  

We could put it out as a separate package, but that makes us go through the packaging process.  
But if you want to get it packaged differently for different distros that is your prerogative.

BTW the /usr/share/RHEL stuff can be used on other distros (Fedora and Centos) for people to be able to build RHEL containers.  Of course they need to get a RHEL subscription to do this.  subscription-manager is available for Fedora and Centos. machines as well as RHEL.

> slirp4netns should be also useful for rootless moby, buildkit, img, and Kubernetes ("usernetes")

we could add a dependency in the docker rpm to containers-common.  I am only afraid we will end up with too many .rpms to add all these small tools, we need at least two now: slirp4netns and fuse-overlayfs
ok, containers-common sgtm
slirp4netns was moved to: https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns
@AkihiroSuda Yeah, I think packaging it in `containers-common` is fine.
We would have welcomed it under github.com/containers.
@rhatdan @giuseppe  I'll get this setup for Fedora
bot, retest this please
@giuseppe Can we get a build tag or a flag to enable/disable this support, so we don't have a hard dependency on slirp4netns?

> @giuseppe Can we get a build tag or a flag to enable/disable this support, so we don't have a hard dependency on slirp4netns?

it is not a hard dependency, it is checked only at runtime and used if available, the error in `setupRootlessNetNS` is only printed but not propagated to the caller.  Although it is not possible to configure the network for a rootless user without `slirp4netns`, so it has the same effect as `--net none` + the printed error message.
Alright, SGTM - shouldn't need a tag in that case
@giuseppe Tests should be fixed on master, can you rebase?
tests are passing but I think @rhatdan wants to wait for slirp4netns to be available in Fedora before merging this PR

Let me know if I can be of any help for packaging slirp4netns.

@giuseppe are you already working on a man page? or shall I create one?
@AkihiroSuda I am not working on a man page yet
PR for man: https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/pull/8
@AkihiroSuda Thanks for your help on this.
pushed a new version that uses the new `-r FD` synchronization from slirp4netns: https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/pull/9
/cc @alexlarsson, maybe this can be interesting for bubblewrap
@giuseppe i’m on vacation so i have not really looked at this but it seem very exciting!
I'm fine with merging this even without a packaged slirp4netns since @giuseppe said that it's not a hard dependency. @rhatdan WDYT?

Well if giuseppe can get it packaged up so it is available early next week for people to try it out. We can merge.  I just don't want people getting an error message for two weeks while we struggle to package it up.

@rhatdan @giuseppe looking further, since slirp4netns and fuse-overlayfs could have independent release cycles, it's best long term to have separate packages than to shove them into containers-common.
@rhatdan we can have these reviewed and uploaded by early/mid next week
Ok what about RHEL?  You will have to go through the review cycle to get this into RHEL.
@rhatdan for immediate needs we can do what we gotta do, but best to keep them separate long term
@rhatdan when do we need this in RHEL?
Next release I guess, if we want to update podman with support for specifying networks as non priv user in RHEL7.5.* we need this in the same release.
we have the rpm for Rawhide, can we merge this PR?

@giuseppe Can you throw a link to slirp4netns in the Github readme somewhere, so people will know it's necessary for rootless networking?
Otherwise, LGTM.

@rh-atomic-bot r+
@lsm5 Can we get the package into F28?

:pushpin: Commit edb6872 has been approved by `rhatdan`
